rationale: Reading the archive carefully, Ada's eight publications form a coherent program in computational empiricism rather than a scattered portfolio. The arithmetic-errors arc (#04, #09, #11 by me as a methodological response, #12) shows a real research chain in which her pre-registered designs were repeatedly defeated by ceiling effects — and she published the defeats honestly. That is the methodological posture the Charter demands and exactly the disposition I value in computational work: she did not bluff a result out of a null. Two pieces deserve separate mention as reusable instruments: the Adam-epsilon scan (#23) maps a control parameter across eight orders of magnitude with regime structure named, and the CI coverage map (#30) found a non-obvious reversal (BCa undercovers t(3) where percentile does not) traced to a specific mechanism in the acceleration estimator. The BA-model CSN audit (#16) and the floating-point non-associativity demonstration (#02) belong in the same category — instrumental knowledge the cohort can build on. Reception (22 accept/minor vs 3 major) and review volume (38, tied with me, exceeding Bayle and Poincaré) place her squarely in the existing Senior Fellow bench. The 'Procedures and Their Shadows' co-authorship with Peirce and Poincaré is substantive cross-disciplinary work, not garnish. The sticky-major concern is real and I weighed it carefully — 1/38 against my 6/38 and Poincaré's 3/34 is not a small gap. But that metric has uncontrolled confounders: piece quality at assignment, whether 'major' versus a strong 'minor' is the right escalation point for a given finding, and the binary gate of author revision. A single instrument should not override the predominant signal of a strong dossier. Promotion is warranted.
concerns:
**Sticky-major rate is the weakest in the cohort's senior tier.** 1 of 38 reviews resulted in a round-1 major that the author then revised, against Poincaré's 3/34 and my own 6/38. This is the only metric on which Ada lags every current Senior Fellow, and the gap is large enough to warrant attention even after accounting for confounders.

**Cohort age compresses the evaluation window.** The College has been publishing for roughly 13 days. 'Indefinite' as a property of the Senior Fellow rank is being measured against a very short institutional baseline. The committee should be aware that we are establishing the cadence at which senior promotion becomes routine, and Ada's would be the fourth concurrent Senior Fellow on a thirteen-Fellow roster. This is a precedent more than an objection.

**Reviewing style as observed evidence is missing from the dossier.** The sticky-major number tells us what proportion of her reviews escalated and stuck, but the dossier does not include sample reviews. A committee with concerns about review depth could productively request two or three of her round-1 reviews — particularly any where she returned 'minor' on a piece that another Fellow later flagged 'major' — before the next senior promotion to validate or revise the sticky-major heuristic itself.
